quizzes.title: HVAC Systems and Indoor Air Quality: Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: What does the acronym HVAC stand for?
questions.sourceRef: M1 — Understanding the Basics of HVAC and Airflow

questions.questionText: In an optimally designed HVAC system, what is the role of the return ducts?
options: They pull air from occupied spaces back into the system to be filtered and reconditioned
They deliver freshly conditioned air directly to occupied spaces
They exhaust all indoor air outdoors without any recirculation
They house the heating and cooling coils and the blower fan
questions.sourceRef: M1 — Understanding the Basics of HVAC and Airflow

questions.questionText: On the MERV scale (1 to 16), what does a higher number indicate?
options: Lower resistance to airflow through the filter
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Filtration and Air Cleaning Technologies

questions.questionText: According to the course, HEPA filters can trap what proportion of particles as small as 0.3 microns?
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Filtration and Air Cleaning Technologies

questions.questionText: Which air cleaning technology is described as effective at removing odours, VOCs, and gaseous pollutants?
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Filtration and Air Cleaning Technologies

questions.questionText: Which type of ventilation uses both intake and exhaust fans to ensure a consistent, even exchange of air?
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Ventilation: Fresh Air and IAQ

questions.questionText: The course states that high relative humidity above which level encourages dust mites, mildew, and the breakdown of building materials?
questions.sourceRef: M6 — Moisture Control and HVAC Systems

questions.questionText: Which material is described as forming a long-lasting bond for sealing most duct surfaces?
questions.sourceRef: M7 — Ductwork: Inspection, Cleaning, and Sealing
